text:
There was an old, wise fox. People often spoke about him; he had seen many things. One day, a pack of pups approached him. They were curious, with big-eyed wonder at everything.

"You've seen a lot," the eldest pup remarked.

The fox chuckled softly. "Oh yes, little ones. I've seen everything!"

"What's there to see?"

"The stars," the fox replied thoughtful.

"The moon"? He couldn’t believe his ears.

"The universe, yes. There are galaxies out there—the Milky Way is lovely. And there are countless stars."

"But they don't have stories?"

"A story? Why—I... I've heard many tales."

One might gather that tales, like stars, could shine quite brightly. The fox pondered a moment. "Perhaps in some ways. There's courage, friendship... love. These are stories too."

The pups listened intently as their wise old friend spun tales of adventure until he finally curled up, a satisfied little flicker of
